title: Map Reset
description: A Mod where you can reset you scaned Map
body:
# MapReset Mod Documentation
Welcome to the MapReset Mod for Minecraft 1.21.11 (Fabric). This mod is designed to save specific areas of a world (e.g., minigame arenas) and restore them to their original state instantly via command.

## 🪄 The Main Tool: The Reset Wand
To mark areas, you need the MapReset Wand.

## Appearance:
It looks like a Blaze Rod.
How to get it: Use the command /mapreset wand or find it in the "Tools & Utilities" Creative tab.
## Usage:
Set Position 1: Left-click on any block.
Feedback: You will hear a "pling" sound, and a green message will appear above your hotbar.
Set Position 2: Right-click on any block.
Feedback: A "chime" sound will play, and a blue message will appear above your hotbar.
Visualization: Once both positions are set, small white particles (End Rod particles) will appear at the corners of the selected area as long as you are holding the wand.
## 💻 Commands:
All features are controlled via the main command /mapreset.

### Command	Description:
/mapreset wand	Gives you the magical MapReset Wand.
/mapreset scan	Saves the currently selected area as a template.
/mapreset reset	Restores the saved area to its original state.
## 🛠️ How it Works (Background)
Marking: The mod stores the coordinates separately for every player.
Scan: When scanning, a file is created in your world folder (/saves/WORLDNAME/mapreset/reset_template.dat). This file contains all block data for the area.
Reset: During a reset, all blocks in the target area are cleared and replaced by the blocks from the template. This process is extremely fast and optimized to prevent server lag.
## 🎨 Features & Quality of Life
Actionbar Feedback: All important information is displayed directly above your hotbar, so you don't miss it in the chat.
Audio Feedback: Special sounds confirm every action, providing acoustic confirmation.
Particle Guide: Particles help you see exactly which area is currently selected.
Combat Protection: While holding the wand, you cannot accidentally hit mobs or players (attacks are blocked), ensuring it functions purely as a tool.
Have fun building and resetting your maps! 🚀
